page: Amazon Web Services c8i.32xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is provisioned with 256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all placing in the top 10% of tested instances. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth, reaching 392.84 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core results falling in the middle 50%. However, the hardware excels in parallelized workloads, showing top-tier capabilities in Redis SET operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression.

The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware. The presence of Intel AMX instructions enables top-tier performance in CPU-based machine learning tasks, such as prompt processing for medium and large language models. Given the compute-heavy profile, high memory bandwidth, and lack of local storage, the c8i.32xlarge is highly suited for high-performance computing (HPC), batch processing, CPU-based AI inference, and intensive web serving workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The CPU supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), which drive top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, cryptographic operations, and CPU-based LLM inference. While single-core performance is average, its multi-threaded capabilities, 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and high memory bandwidth position it in the top 10% for parallel processing. Lacking local storage and dedicated GPUs, this instance is highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads, including high-performance computing, software compilation, and large-scale web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.32xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, Intel Xeon processors, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.32xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for demanding parallel processing and CPU-based inference workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
256.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory with 480 MB L3 cache
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Top-tier multi-core benchmark performance and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or dedicated GPUs, optimizing cost for pure compute workloads
Best suited for high-performance computing, CPU-based LLM inference, and batch processing
